items.title: Foden signs new four-year Man City contract
items.summary: 26-year-old looking forward to working with Maresca ‘To stay this long at the club is a dream really,’ he says Phil Foden has signed a new four-year contract with Manchester City, saying he is looking forward to “trying to win more” at his boyhood club. The fresh deal, which has an additional 12-month option, ties the 26-year-old to the club until 2030 and he is is relishing working again with Enzo Maresca. The new City manager was Pep Guardiola’s assistant during the 2022-23 treble-winning season.

items.title: Southampton boss Eckert charged by FA over Spygate
items.summary: 33-year-old German could be hit with one-year ban South-coast club denied chance to win promotion Tonda Eckert has been charged with misconduct by the Football Association for orchestrating the spying scandal that saw Southampton being kicked out of the Championship playoff final . The FA announced action against the 33-year-old German, who has accepted “full responsibility” for sending club staff to observe training sessions of three opponents, on Wednesday, confirming he is facing three counts of misconduct and has until 28 July to provide responses.

items.title: Chelsea sign £117m Rogers - what will he bring?
items.summary: England international signs seven-year contract Xabi Alonso was key factor in Chelsea’s record signing Morgan Rogers has joined Chelsea from Aston Villa for a fee of £117m, signing a seven-year deal to become the Blues’ record signing. A deal was agreed on Saturday for the 23-year-old to move to Stamford Bridge as the club stole in ahead of Arsenal, who had been in talks to sign the England international and had appeared to be favourites to secure his signature.

items.title: Financial exceptions could be made for Guardiola - Italian FA
items.summary: Italian football federation confirm interest in Guardiola Mancini and Pirlo also linked with national team vacancy Financial “exceptions” are in place to allow Pep Guardiola to become Italy’s next head coach, the president of the Italian Football Federation (FIGC), Giovanni Malago, has said. Guardiola left Manchester City at the end of last season after winning six Premier League titles, three FA Cups, five League Cups and the Champions League during a hugely successful 10-year reign. He is free to take up another job, having also coached Barcelona and Bayern Munich, and has been linked with the Italy vacancy for some time, with it reported earlier this week that he has held talks with the FIGC.

items.title: 'Pressure is intense and scrutiny constant' - referee Taylor retires
items.summary: The 47-year-old took charge of five World Cup matches Taylor has refereed in the Premier League for 16 years Anthony Taylor has announced his retirement from elite refereeing after a distinguished career spanning more than two decades and 831 matches. One of England’s most accomplished officials, the 47-year-old officiated 668 matches in the professional game in England. He brings the curtain down on his career following his final appointment – the World Cup last‑16 game between Portugal and Spain in the US.

items.title: ‘Reclaim football’: Sepp Blatter joins La Liga chief in call for Gianni Infantino to resign
items.summary: Blatter attacks successor as Fifa president Javier Tebas says system is ‘rotten at its core’ The Fifa president, Gianni Infantino, has come in for heavy criticism from his predecessor, Sepp Blatter, and the La Liga president, Javier Tebas, with the pair united in the belief that he should stand down. As Spain celebrated their World Cup win on Sunday, Tebas accused Infantino of “destroying the football industry” by prioritising the World Cup over national competitions. Blatter has called for “fans, players and national associations to reclaim football and bring it back to its roots under new leadership”.

items.title: Italy willing to make 'exceptional' budget change to hire Guardiola
items.summary: The Italian Football Federation (FIGC) has met with Pep Guardiola to convince him to take over as national team head coach and are willing to make an exceptional adjustment to their budget to hire him

items.title: World Cup, Ballon d’Or, Champions League, Euros … has Rodri completed football? | The Knowledge
items.summary: Plus: the most World Cup assists, Ballon d’Or winners with fewest caps and what happens to World Cup balls? Mail us with your all of your questions and answers “Adding the World Cup to his résumé means Rodri has now won the World Cup, the European Championship, the Champions League, and the Ballon d’Or,” writes Owen Bagnall. “Has anyone else ‘completed football’ like this?” They have but it’s a small, elite list.

items.title: Football Daily | Here are 48 reasons to cherish the World Cup (minus the Geopolitics)
items.summary: The Geopolitics World Cup is already fading from view, replaced by a thin July gruel of pre-season friendlies and transfer tales. But there’s still time to cast another look back over the 48-team, 104-game, 308-goal leviathan that has dominated the last six weeks of our lives. Without further ado, here are 48 reasons to cherish this year’s World Cup (minus the Geopolitics).

items.title: ‘The utmost respect for him’: Alex Ferguson pays tribute to Kevin Keegan
items.summary: Ferguson says Keegan’s Newcastle was their best team ‘Kevin carried the love of the game on his sleeve’ Sir Alex Ferguson has saluted Kevin Keegan for building Newcastle’s “greatest team”. Keegan, who had cancer, died on Monday at the age of 75 and Ferguson has joined the tributes for one of football’s most endearing characters . Ferguson and Keegan were involved a fierce battle for Premier League supremacy, with Ferguson’s Manchester United pipping Newcastle to the title in 1996.

items.title: ‘Where did they go?’: homeless people feel force of America’s brutality in World Cup clean-up
items.summary: Fifa says football brings the world together but the unhoused in Atlanta feel targeted and completely excluded from the tournament “A lot of our community has been pushed out by the World Cup. We’re not just dollar signs, we’re more than that. We’re people and we’re frustrated that they’ve chosen to treat us less than human.” “ They dropped me off there in the middle of the night.

items.summary: Nu är sommaruppehållet äntligen slut och den riktiga fotbollen börjar igen. Huvudrätten under söndagen blir matchen på Parken mot Stålmännen från Sandviken. Bruksorten är platsen där vår tränare Eldars tillbringat större delen av sitt liv, och där han blev formad som tränare.

items.title: Krönika: Mäster Skräddare
items.summary: Denna artikel är byggd kring den klassiska historien ”Mäster Skräddare”, där en man går till skräddaren för att få en rock sydd. Det blir dock ingen rock, utan för varje vecka han kommer dit så blir plagget mindre och mindre, tills det inte blir något alls. Den artikel skall läsas med STOR ironi, då den inte är menad att skada någon på något sätt utan bara få er läsare att tänka till och kanske, eventuellt, få ett gott skratt

items.title: A tournament that crossed borders: crunching the numbers on the diaspora World Cup
items.summary: Nationality in football has never been so complex and the influence of players from the diaspora has never been higher at a World Cup “Nationality says where you come from. And I actually come from four countries: France, Algeria, Nigeria and Great Britain. I consider myself very lucky to possess these four parts, which all enrich me.” This view was given by Michael Olise.

items.title: World Cup 2030 (very early) power rankings: who will challenge Spain at top?
items.summary: The next men’s World Cup is still four years away, but we take a look at the teams with the firmest foundations who should be primed to contend The 2026 World Cup provided many an esteemed veteran with what could be their international swansongs, with golden greats Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo, Luka Modric, Neymar, James Rodríguez and Guillermo Ochoa all making teary exits as their tournament ended. As we turn ahead to the 2030 World Cup, any player 32 or older will be sweating their selection status and changing their daily routines to stay sharp against inevitable aging. For these power rankings, we’re overvaluing sure things.

items.title: Why many Black Americans were rooting for Argentina to lose the World Cup | Etan Thomas
items.summary: Many Black Americans were not cheering Spain’s complicated history. They were rooting against the racism, politics and silence associated with Argentina There are moments in sports when you realize people aren’t just watching the score any more. And they’re watching what those silences say.

items.title: David Squires on … Spain winning the World Cup final in the shadow of Trump

items.title: A big red speaker, Louis Vuitton trophy trunks and a tattoo bet – how Spain celebrated World Cup win
items.summary: Luis de la Fuente’s team was the best at the tournament by some margin but are not satisfied yet with focus now on ‘the next game in September’ On the night that Spain won their first World Cup in 2010, Rodrigo Hernández was on camp somewhere lost in the woods of Connecticut. He had been canoeing when the selección beat Germany in the semi-final, upset to have missed it, and for the final he convinced one of the supervisors to let him watch, alone, on a mini-laptop in the hut where the staff slept. When Andrés Iniesta scored the winning goal in the 116th minute, he ran off screaming, doing laps of the lake.

items.title: Spain win World Cup after Ferran Torres breaks 10-man Argentina’s resistance
items.summary: It was the triumph of an idea, an identity. Of an unshakable and patient faith. And when it was over, Spain’s new generation having emulated the one from the 2010 World Cup, all of those legendary names, there was simply delirium for them.

items.title: 3 punkter efter 0-0 mot Djurgården
items.summary: 8697 åskådare hade tagit sig till Gamla Ullevi för att se ÖIS ta emot Djurgården. En match som många på förhand trodde skulle bli en målrik historia blev något helt annat och slutade istället 0-0. ÖIS gjorde en bra insats försvarsmässigt och lyckades neutralisera den offensiv som Djurgården visat upp i tidigare matcher.
